prompt: Our load-test CLI keeps printing empty reports: queue 500 virtual users, Fire(), and the latency histogram says 0 samples — but the target service definitely received the requests, we can see them in its access log a moment later. So Fire is coming back before the burst has actually finished, even though it's documented to block until all tasks complete. Occasionally we get a partial histogram instead of an empty one, and once I saw a panic out of the sync package. It's in burst.go. Fix it without touching burst_test.go and prove it with `go test -race -timeout 30s ./...` (run it a few times, this one has a habit of 'passing on my machine').
